frameworks.name: chronic Neuropixels electrophysiology
frameworks.one_line: long-term multi-probe extracellular recording in freely moving rats

core_question: Can Neuropixels probes be chronically implanted, reused, and recorded from over months in freely moving rats?
key_assumptions: probe reuse is feasible if explant noise stays below single-unit threshold
yield can be assessed across anatomical positions
manipulations: probe reuse across multiple implantation cycles
number of probes implanted per rat
key_findings: hundreds of units recorded for multiple months in unrestrained rats
yields depended systematically on anatomical position
explanted probes showed small noise increase versus unimplanted
noise increase insufficient to impair future single-unit recordings
interpretations: cost-effective multi-region multi-probe Neuropixels chronic recording is viable in rats
approach may facilitate standardization of chronic Neuropixels recordings in freely moving animals
limitations_noted: long-term stability and probe reusability were previously unaddressed in initial studies
